root_text.an5.195:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā vesāliyaṁ viharati mahāvane kūṭāgārasālāyaṁ.
root_text.an5.195:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena pañcamattāni licchavisatāni bhagavantaṁ payirupāsanti.
root_text.an5.195:1.3: Appekacce licchavī nīlā honti nīlavaṇṇā nīlavatthā nīlālaṅkārā, appekacce licchavī pītā honti pītavaṇṇā pītavatthā pītālaṅkārā, appekacce licchavī lohitakā honti lohitakavaṇṇā lohitakavatthā lohitakālaṅkārā, appekacce licchavī odātā honti odātavaṇṇā odātavatthā odātālaṅkārā.
root_text.an5.195:1.4: Tyassudaṁ bhagavā atirocati vaṇṇena ceva yasasā ca.
root_text.an5.195:2.1: Atha kho piṅgiyānī brāhmaṇo uṭṭhāyāsanā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā yena bhagavā tenañjaliṁ paṇāmetvā bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an5.195:2.4: Atha kho piṅgiyānī brāhmaṇo bhagavato sammukhā sāruppāya gāthāya abhitthavi:
root_text.an5.195:4.1: Atha kho te licchavī pañcahi uttarāsaṅgasatehi piṅgiyāniṁ brāhmaṇaṁ acchādesuṁ.
root_text.an5.195:4.2: Atha kho piṅgiyānī brāhmaṇo tehi pañcahi uttarāsaṅgasatehi bhagavantaṁ acchādesi.
root_text.an5.195:5.1: Atha kho bhagavā te licchavī etadavoca:
root_text.an5.195:5.4: Tathāgatassa arahato sammāsambuddhassa pātubhāvo dullabho lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an5.195:5.5: Tathāgatappaveditassa dhammavinayassa desetā puggalo dullabho lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an5.195:5.6: Tathāgatappaveditassa dhammavinayassa desitassa viññātā puggalo dullabho lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an5.195:5.7: Tathāgatappaveditassa dhammavinayassa desitassa viññātā dhammānudhammappaṭipanno puggalo dullabho lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an5.195:5.9: Imesaṁ kho, licchavī, pañcannaṁ ratanānaṁ pātubhāvo dullabho lokasmin”ti.

translation_text.an5.195:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Vesālī, at the Great Wood, in the hall with the peaked roof.
translation_text.an5.195:1.2: Now at that time around five hundred Licchavis were visiting the Buddha.
translation_text.an5.195:1.3: Some of the Licchavis were in blue, of blue color, clad in blue, adorned with blue. And some were similarly colored in yellow, red, or white.
translation_text.an5.195:1.4: But the Buddha outshone them all in beauty and glory.
translation_text.an5.195:2.1: Then the brahmin Piṅgīyānī got up from his seat, arranged his robe over one shoulder, raised his joined palms toward the Buddha, and said,
translation_text.an5.195:2.2: “I feel inspired to speak, Blessed One! I feel inspired to speak, Holy One!”
translation_text.an5.195:2.3: “Then speak as you feel inspired,” said the Buddha.
translation_text.an5.195:2.4: So the brahmin Piṅgīyānī extolled the Buddha in his presence with a fitting verse.
translation_text.an5.195:3.2: that blooms at daybreak, its fragrance unfaded—
translation_text.an5.195:3.4: bright as the sun in the sky!”
translation_text.an5.195:4.1: Then those Licchavis clothed Piṅgiyānī with five hundred upper robes.
translation_text.an5.195:4.2: And Piṅgiyānī clothed the Buddha with them.
translation_text.an5.195:5.1: Then the Buddha said to the Licchavis:
translation_text.an5.195:5.2: “Licchavis, the appearance of five treasures is rare in the world.
translation_text.an5.195:5.4: A Realized One, a perfected one, a fully awakened Buddha.
translation_text.an5.195:5.5: A teacher of the teaching and training proclaimed by a Realized One.
translation_text.an5.195:5.6: An individual who understands the teaching and training proclaimed by a Realized One.
translation_text.an5.195:5.7: An individual who practices in line with the teaching.
translation_text.an5.195:5.8: An individual who is grateful and thankful.
translation_text.an5.195:5.9: The appearance of these five treasures is rare in the world.”

variant_text.an5.195:3.1: Padmaṁ → padumaṁ (pts1ed, mr) | kokanadaṁ → kokanudaṁ (sya-all, km)
